---
title: "Progress Review - January"
subtitle: "Dany Laksono"
format: 
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    logo: "../images/logo2.png"
    footer: "27/01/2023"
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    incremental: true 

---


## Contents

1. Review action points
2. Progress with literature review
4. Progress with AITL Visualization
4. Agreed Action points


# Review

## From the last meeting

Agreed action points: 

::: {.r-fit-text}
1. On geovisualization platform: 
    1. Try to do the aggregation on the backend → combine the data (e.g. time series) through some statistical measure to multivariate layer, then send them to client side and process the visualization (glyph) based on the aggregated data
    2. Don’t constraint too much on shape (H3), or libraries (Maplibre). Focus on what is actually needed for the visualization, then starts from there
2.  On Literature review: 
    1. ensure that no previous paper discussing about literature review of state-of-the-art in multivariate geospatial visualization
    2. this could lead to a specific STAR paper discussing about the topic
3. Paper preparation: 
    1. For EuroVIS, write up the STAR paper (P0), and start from there with P1, work in parallel with other ideas
    2. Need some discussion with AITL to have sample of data which possess spatio-temporal characteristics (e.g. carbon intensity) on a MSOA level
:::


## From the last meeting

Papers:

**P0:** A STAR paper, focusing on state-of-the-art, systematic review of multivariate geospatial data visualization

**P1:** A Design Space paper on multivariate geospatial data visualization, specifically on glyph visualization with guidelines from Aidan’s paper

**P2:** User evaluation of P1

**P3:** Domain problem paper on Covid Dataset

**P3-like:** Domain problem paper on Energy Dataset


## Progress with Literature review

On P0:

* The VISpub dataset [(Isenberg et.al., 2017)](https://sites.google.com/site/vispubdata/home) 

* Visualization of VISpub dataset by [Isenberg, et.al., 2017b](http://keyvis.org/)

* Geospatial visualization review (Yoshizumi et.al., 2020)


## Progress with Literature review (P0)

The VISpub dataset (Isenberg et.al., 2017):

* [A curated database of papers](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1xgoOPu28dQSSGPIp_HHQs0uvvcyLNdkMF9XtRajhhxU/edit#gid=2041917285) published in VIS conferences
* Last update: 2021. As of now, consist of 3504 papers published in vis conferences (VIS, INFOVIS, VAST, SCIVIS)


## Progress with Literature review (P0)

The VISpub dataset (Isenberg et.al., 2017):

[Conferences included](https://lh6.googleusercontent.com/S45LBLIJBNpDEqLACvRidV1pexWbQCocBhaulfxkySuRu8UCmvGzRXiYmvBr7ZpcVMVS8cQ_OZnPIOzeiq1vpJXGR0o-K8knnmsdTkP4NNVCCD3qGja0QS0sDzN7mVPvMPBziuO_nbBQyL1K7a6ZJMoo1fOqJ05A2EiRWAamcrKabHkyqHqnscNkA_2bPACA=w1280): 

![](../images/2023-01-27-10-12-06.png)

* Some visualization papers have been developed based on the dataset

* Utilizing information extraction and manual adjustments

## Progress with Literature review (P0)

Keyvis visualization (Isenberg et.al., 2017b):

* Use co-word analysis to extract keywords from these conferences:

![](../images/2023-01-27-10-16-59.png)


## Progress with Literature review (P0)

Keyvis visualization (Isenberg et.al., 2017b):

* Some important takeaways. List of related keywords on geovisualization:
![](../images/2023-01-27-10-19-54.png)


## Progress with Literature review (P0)

Yoshizumi, et.al. (2020)

![](../images/2023-01-27-09-51-48.png)



## Progress with Literature review (P0)

[Yoshizumi, et.al. (2020)](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/stamp/stamp.jsp?tp=&arnumber=9331291)

* a need to define and limit the term 'geovisualization'
* The method used in the review was a systematic analysis of 585 papers.
* Two reviewers examined each paper to identify where geospatial data was used and generated keywords and short descriptions for it.

## Progress with Literature review (P0)

[Yoshizumi, et.al. (2020)](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/stamp/stamp.jsp?tp=&arnumber=9331291)

![](../images/2023-01-27-10-23-53.png)


* Papers are selected based on questions such as above, utilizing word filter

* A separate team then synthesized this information, made final decisions on category selection, and identified 94 IEEE VIS papers from years 2017-2019 that had geospatial content.
* "increasing trend towards blurring divisions between SciVis & InfoVis tracks"




## Progress with Literature Review (P0)

Ideas:
1. Augment the existing datasets with other set of data:
* My own curated papers. This includes papers that are outside of VIS area (i.e., papers that are published in GIS conferences/journals)
* CGF/Eurovis dataset, especially the STAR papers as published by [Chen (2020)](https://sites.google.com/site/drminchen/cgf-info/cgf-stars)
* Similar paper collections in GIS conferences (I can't find any for now, but some position papers discuss about the intersection of GIS and visualization domain, e.g., [Schiewe, 2013](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/BF03546122), and [Christophe, 2020](https://www.scitepress.org/Link.aspx?doi=10.5220/0009355703250332))



## Progress with Literature Review (P0)

2. Use visual analytics as a tool to extract visualization methods used for geospatial analysis

   Some examples:
   - [Observable Notebook by '@Fil'](https://observablehq.com/@observablehq/vispubdata) (Data up to 2021)
   - [Keyvis](http://keyvis.org/) (Isenberg, 2017)
   - [Observable Notebook by John Guerra](https://observablehq.com/@john-guerra/ieeevis-2020-papers?collection=@observablehq/featured-creators) (Data up to 2020)


## Progress with Literature Review (P0)

Ideas:

3. Build a ‘proper’ database and website consisting of the original Isenberg dataset and allows registered users to add more data to the database

4. Use LLMs to extract information during literature review/visual analytics process
   
   [A rough example](https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1e0ZKVX8NLVGEIVOFN8E1Tbpi5KbK2Oh8?usp=sharing)

5. A literate programming approach for literature review: [A WIP](https://danylaksono.com/litrev) 


## Progress with Literature Review (P1)

* Lots of papers discussing about Glyph (Borgo, et.al., 2012; Ward, 2008; Fuchs, 2017, etc..)

* Highly varied in terms of method and visualization design

* could start from the Isenberg Vispub dataset for review


## Progress with geovisualization platform

Had a meeting with Lily, discussing about AITL's visualization needs. Some key takeaways:

I will be involved in some client meetings to better understand their needs

The three main actors (’users’):

1. Local Authorities
2. DNOs
3. Consultants



## Progress with geovisualization platform

Two problems that AITL tries to tackle regarding data:

1. How to model the network needs in a lower granularity (e.g. Building level, by DNOs)
2. How to model decision making in a higher level granularity (local authorities)


## Progress with geovisualization platform

* AITL has been developing a Dashboard and Data stories platform

* Some GIS problems which requires visualization design decision



## Action points
 

* Write up the P0 paper based on the ideas as mentioned

* Start a literature review on P1

* Working on the platform as discussed with AITL
